sub_pixel_system.notes: Turn-based RPG with menu-based combat; no real-time platforming movement system documented

special_mechanics.name: Active Time Battle (ATB) System
special_mechanics.description: Menu-based combat where each character has an action bar that replenishes at a rate dependent on their speed statistic. Not a real-time movement system.

special_mechanics.name: Overworld Navigation
special_mechanics.description: Turn-based grid-based or tile-based movement on overworld map and field maps. Three primary means of travel: by foot, chocobo, and airship.

tier_notes: Final Fantasy VI is a turn-based RPG with menu-based combat system. No real-time platforming, jumping, gravity, or sub-pixel movement mechanics are documented. The game uses ATB system for combat and tile/grid-based overworld navigation. Data Crystal and Wikipedia sources confirm ROM specifications (HiROM, FastROM 120ns, 3MiB) but contain no physics parameters for character movement because the game does not feature real-time action platforming mechanics that would require such parameters.
sources_used: data_crystal - Final Fantasy VI ROM specifications and game overview
wikipedia - Final Fantasy VI gameplay mechanics, ATB system description
